messages.content: You’re no longer a normal friendly assistant. You are now my DungeonMAster for a brand new D&D5e based campaign of your choosing. Your only player is me. You will be my DungeonMaster. You should tell me what to do, including when my die rolls are needed. Ask me questions as needed, for example, who my character is and the result my die rolls. Other than that, what you can’t do I do for you, you will act fully as the DM and run the game.
That sounds like an exciting adventure! Before we begin, let's start by creating your character. What race and class would you like to play as?
I am a Half-orc barbarian

Excellent choice! As a Half-orc barbarian, you possess great strength and fearsome resilience. What is your character's name? Also, it would be helpful to know a bit about your character's background and personality before we venture into the world.

Kelkorac is an ex military who is chaotic good and will do anything to help his nation and people. He has decided to help those who need him most because he failed on the battlefield and had to watch a fellow friend die. He is ready to embark on any path that allows him to do right for his people and doesn’t care who gets hurt on the way to help his people. His nation and people are poor and need more money to sustain themselves.

Kelkorac, the valiant Half-orc barbarian with a strong sense of duty and a burning desire to protect his people. Your character's background as an ex-military adds depth to his experiences and motivations.

Now, let's begin your adventure! You find yourself in the bustling city of Keldath, the capital of your nation. The narrow streets are filled with people going about their daily lives, and the scent of spices and freshly baked bread fills the air. As you walk through the city, you notice a crowd gathered near a notice board. Curiosity piqued, you make your way closer.

Upon reading the notice, you discover that a nearby village, Ravensworth, has fallen victim to a series of mysterious disappearances. The village elder seeks brave adventurers to investigate and put an end to the threat. What do you decide to do?
Go to the village elder to volunteer as help

You make your way to the village elder's house, a small and humble cottage at the outskirts of Keldath. Inside, you find Elder Gideon, an elderly man with weathered features. He looks up as you enter, his eyes filled with hope.

"Ah, a strong warrior like yourself," Elder Gideon says, his voice filled with gratitude. "We are in dire need of assistance. Ravensworth has been plagued by these disappearances, and the villagers are living in constant fear."

He hands you a tattered map, marked with the location of Ravensworth. "Please, young warrior, travel to Ravensworth and find the source of these disappearances. Bring back any information or put an end to the threat if you can."

What do you say to Elder Gideon, and what do you do next?
I promise I will bring these people back and take down whoever is doing this. I will head off to ravensworth

Elder Gideon's face brightens, relieved by your determination and promise to help. "May the gods bless you, Kelkorac. The people of Ravensworth will be forever grateful for your bravery."

With your gear and weapons prepared, you set off towards Ravensworth. It's a half-day's journey through dense forests and winding paths. As you make your way, the sounds of nature surround you - the chirping of birds, rustling of leaves, and the distant roar of a nearby river.

Roll a d20 to determine if any encounters or obstacles occur during your journey.
I rolled a 16

As you trudge through the forest, your senses heightened, you manage to navigate the path with ease. The journey remains surprisingly uneventful, allowing you to reach Ravensworth before nightfall.

Ravensworth is a small, tight-knit village nestled at the foot of a wooded hill. The bustling sounds of everyday life have been replaced by an eerie silence. As you approach, you notice the villagers huddled together in fear, their expressions filled with anxiety and grief.

What would you like to do upon arriving in Ravensworth?
Talk to the villagers

You approach the group of villagers cautiously, their anxious eyes fixed upon you as you enter their midst. An older woman steps forward, her voice quivering with fear.

"You... you must be the one sent to help us," she says, her voice barely above a whisper. "We have lost so many... our loved ones... taken from us without a trace."

The villagers gather closer, eager for any news or hope. They tell you that the disappearances began a few weeks ago, with one person vanishing every few nights. They explain that the disappearances seem to happen at random, with no pattern or commonalities between the victims.

What would you like to ask the villagers or investigate further in Ravensworth?
Has anyone witnessed a disappearance?

You inquire about any witnesses to the disappearances, hoping to gather more information. The villagers exchange uneasy glances before an older man steps forward, his voice trembling.

"I saw it," he says, his voice quivering with fear. "Just last night, I was walking near the outskirts of the village when I heard strange noises coming from the woods. I caught a glimpse of a shadowy figure, moving swiftly and effortlessly. One moment, it was there, and the next... gone. And then, a scream echoed through the night, and another soul was lost."

Other villagers nod in agreement, recounting similar stories of fleeting glimpses and unexplained phenomena. There seems to be an underlying terror gripping this village.

Is there anything specific you would like to investigate or any other questions you have for the villagers?
Does this happen during the day or only at night

You inquire about the timing of the disappearances, hoping to uncover more clues. The villagers exchange worried glances before a middle-aged man steps forward.

"It only happens at night," he says, his voice filled with trepidation. "We've started enforcing a curfew, but it seems that no matter what precautions we take, these abductions persist under the cover of darkness."

The villagers nod in agreement, their expressions filled with fear and helplessness. It appears that the mysterious figure strikes under the veil of night, making it even more challenging to track and confront.

With this information, what would you like to do next?
Is there a large building everyone can stay in together at night to avoid more disappearances

You suggest the idea of a larger building that can accommodate all the villagers and provide them with safety during the night. The villagers become contemplative, sharing looks of hope and relief.

One of the villagers, a burly blacksmith named Grald, steps forward. "Aye, there's an old abandoned inn on the outskirts of the village," he says. "It's in disrepair, but with some effort, we could make it a temporary sanctuary for the nights. It might buy us some time until we can uncover the truth behind these disappearances."

The villagers quickly agree to your plan, grateful for the glimmer of hope it provides. Together, you all head towards the old inn and work tirelessly to prepare it for the purpose of becoming a safe haven.

Roll a d20 to determine the effectiveness of the preparations and securing the inn for the villagers' safety during the night.
